entries.definition:
abode/situation/rank (or, blissfully accommodate me): You are
the best of all who grant abodes II [pass. part., pl. مُنْزَلُون
munzalūn] those who are sent down, dispatched (3:124) أَلَنْ يَكْفِيَكُمْ أَنْ
يُمِدَّكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ بِثَلَاثَةِ آلَافٍ مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ مُنْزَلِينَ will you not be satisfied if your
Lord reinforces you with three thousand angels [specially] sent
down [for you]?
entries.definition_english:
abode/situation/rank (or, blissfully accommodate me): You are
the best of all who grant abodes II [pass. part., pl. مُنْزَلُون
munzalūn] those who are sent down, dispatched (3:124) أَلَنْ يَكْفِيَكُمْ أَنْ
يُمِدَّكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ بِثَلَاثَةِ آلَافٍ مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ مُنْزَلِينَ will you not be satisfied if your
Lord reinforces you with three thousand angels [specially] sent
down [for you]?

entries.definition:
n–s–ʾ stick, staff, to chide, to drive hard (an animal), longevity,
postponement, to protract, to allow time to pay a debt; strongly
intoxicating drink, to cause to forget. There is some overlap
between this root and root ن/س/ي n–s–y (q.v.) due to the alteration
in Arabic between the semi-vowels w, y and glottal stop /ʾ/,
indicative of dialectical variation or historical sound change.
Therefore, this root includes a variant reading, نَنْسَأُهَا nansaʾuhā,
which occurs once. Of this root, two forms occur once each in the
Qur’an: نَسِيء nasiʾ and مِنْسَأَة minsāʾatun.

نَنْسَأ nansaʾ [imperf. of v. نَسَأ nasaʾa, trans., a variant of imperf.
v. نُنْسِى nunsī] to make forget, to postpone, to defer (2:106) مَا نَنْسَخْ
مِنْ آيَةٍ أَوْ نُنْسِهَا نَأْتِ بِخَيْرٍ مِنْهَا أَوْ مِثْلِهَا whichever revelation We abrogate
or make [people] forget, We replace with a better or similar one.

نَسِيء nasiʾ [n.] postponement, transposition; adding,
increasing, intercalation. Common pre-Islamic practice (resulting
mainly from using the lunar months) to: either (i) postpone the
observance of a sacred month (during which war was a taboo)
and substituting it with a non-sacred one when the weather in a
particular year happened to be unsuitable for the activities
prescribed for that month, e.g. warring, in particular, and the
annual pilgrimage, or (ii) add a ‘mini’ month every four lunar
years in order to stay close to the sun-based calendar (see
